creator: guru@unixarea.de
text:
(In reply to Thomas Zander from comment #13)
> (In reply to Matthias Apitz from comment #12)
>  

> > btw: the CFLAGS from Makefile.option only work when one runs
> > 
> > setenv CFLAGS ''
> > make
> 
> Are you saying the CFLAGS from the port Makefile are ignored unless you
> empty the variable prior to build? Are you enforcing CFLAGS=someFlags in
> make.conf? If so, please switch to using CFLAGS+=....

I do not have any CFLAGS in /etc/make.conf; if I do

unsetenv CFLAGS
make

it compiles with 

clang -MMD -MP -D_ISOC99_SOURCE -I. -Iffmpeg -O2 -pipe  -DLIBICONV_PLUG -O3 -fno-strict-aliasing -D_LARGEFILE_SOURCE -D_FILE_OFFSET_BITS=64 -D_FILE_OFFSET_BITS=64 ....

the -O3 comes from Makefile.options, but there is some -O2 before; don't know where this comes from;
